danger_signals.visual_form: Projectiles from enemy units and vehicles
danger_signals.meaning: Immediate threat to player; collision results in loss of life

danger_signals.visual_form: Environmental hazards integrated into level design
danger_signals.meaning: Dangerous environmental elements; collision causes damage

danger_signals.visual_form: Large, distinctly designed enemy units at stage end (e.g., Orcus)
danger_signals.meaning: Significant threat requiring defeat to progress; marks progression gate

danger_signals.visual_form: Large military/mechanized units on scrolling stages
danger_signals.meaning: High-threat mobile obstacles and sources of sustained fire

danger_signals.visual_form: Visual representation of General Akhboob's deformed/mutated soldiers
danger_signals.meaning: Opposition force; primary combat threat throughout levels

safety_signals.visual_form: Captive characters or marked containment areas within levels
safety_signals.meaning: Mission objective; rescuing these advances story and provides completion condition

safety_signals.visual_form: Collectible bomb items placed in environment
safety_signals.meaning: Tactical advantage; can be placed to clear threats and damage enemies

safety_signals.visual_form: Life counter and credit system (arcade machine mechanic)
safety_signals.meaning: Safety net allowing continued play after loss; monetary continuation system

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Captain Carnage and Major Mayhem character designs
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Player-controlled units dominating center of action; protagonist positioning

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Dictatorial military imagery; mutant aesthetic for army forces
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Mutated soldiers; large military vehicles; boss characters like Orcus
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Military base structure (Baby Milk Factory); Kookistan setting; enemy unit placement throughout scrolling levels

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Kookistan territory under regime control; military infrastructure visible; enemy patrols and vehicles present
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Progressive enemy difficulty; increasing numbers of mutant soldiers; stage bosses guard key locations

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Regime's central stronghold; military/industrial base aesthetic; highest concentration of threats and final boss encounter
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Maximum enemy density; final boss Akhboob (implied through structure); completion defeats regime

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Contained prisoners visible in environment; marked holding areas within levels
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Rescue mechanic as primary objective alongside enemy elimination

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Alternate environment separate from military zones; treasure collection opportunity
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Optional/alternate gameplay path; completion state affects ending cinematics (treasure collection changes ending)

environmental_storytelling.example: Kookistan fictional country setting establishes dictatorship context; 1999 timeframe suggests near-future conflict scenario
General Akhboob character and plot loosely based on Saddam Hussein; Abu Ghraib Infant Formula Plant reference encodes 1991 Persian Gulf War context into game narrative
John Rambo-inspired player character design and military commando aesthetic communicates lone-soldier-against-regime narrative without explicit dialogue
Baby Milk Factory final stage base reveals regime's nature through facility purpose; military infrastructure throughout levels shows occupation control
Named boss characters (Orcus) visually distinct and larger than regular mutant soldiers; represent regime's power structure

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Final boss encounter requiring defeat; implied presence throughout levels as dictatorial authority
Patrol and engage players; fire projectiles; increase in number with stage progression
Present in scrolling stages; deliver sustained fire; represent regime military capability
Appear at stage conclusion; require sustained engagement; must be defeated to progress
Remain in fixed containment locations; require player rescue; completion of mission objective
Dual-stick control enables omnidirectional movement and firing; sustained engagement with enemies; progresses through regime territory

notes: Total Carnage uses satirical political naming (Akhboob, Kookistan) to encode real-world conflict (Persian Gulf War, Abu Ghraib) into arcade action context. World-state is communicated primarily through environment design (military base structure, scrolling territories), enemy density and variety (mutants increase with progression), and objective markers (POW locations). The game lacks explicit dialogue or UI-based lore; narrative and faction identity emerge entirely through visual and mechanical design. Bonus level (Pleasure Dome) represents alternate/aspirational state with treasure collecting mechanic distinct from combat focus.
